terms.definition: prescribed, regulated, bound by cosmic/dharmic order; duty not chosen but inherited through circumstance and station
action, work, ritual performance; specifically niyata-karma: duty performed without attachment to result
superior, better, more excellent; comparative of jya (to overpower) — the moral hierarchy between performed duty and renunciatory evasion
non-action, inaction, renunciation without qualification; the false renunciation of the immature practitioner
bodily maintenance, journey of the body (sharira: that which decays + yatra: going) — the minimum requirement of prakriti that cannot be evaded
would be accomplished, effected, made successful; from pra-sidh (to execute/complete)
